sections.paragraph:
constr. מִשְׁפַּחַת, with suff. מִשְׁפַּחְתִּי, pl. מִשְׁפָּחֹות Psa 107:41, constr. and with suff. מִשְׁפְּחֹות f. (from the root שָׁפַח , which see).

(1) genus, kind (of animals), Gen 8:19 also used of inanimate things, Jer 15:3.

(2) gens, tribe, Gen 10:18, 20 Gen 10:20, 31 Gen 10:31, 32 Gen 10:32 12:3 also used of a whole people, Eze 20:32 Jer 8:3 25:9 Mic 2:3. In the subdivisions of the people of Israel-

(3) in a narrower sense, it denotes specially a family, of which several were comprehended in one tribe (שֵׁבֶט), so on the other hand one family included several fathers’ houses (בֵּית אָבֹות see p. 116, B ), Exo 6:14, seq.; Num 1:2, 20 , seq.; 26:5 , seq.; Deu 29:17 Jos 7:14, seq.; 21:5 , seq.; 1Sa 20:29, זֶבַח מִשְׁפָּחָה לָנוּ “we have a family sacrifice.” It is sometimes less exactly put for שֵׁבֶט as Jos 7:17, מִשְׁפַּחַת יְהוּדָה for the preceding שֵׁבֶט יְהוּדָה.
